paragraphs.body: Joshua Novak an American philosopher once stated, “Love is not a feeling of happiness. Love is a willingness to sacrifice.” This statement made me think a lot about the meaning of life. How does someone show they love someone. Is it giving someone hugs or kisses. Is it buying them gifts or giving them something they want. Or is it to make someone happy.

annotations.text: Or is it to make someone happy.
annotations.comment: Interesting...can anyone make anyone else happy? And how is this related to sacrifice?

annotations.text: Or is it to make someone happy.

paragraphs.body: One of the best days of my life was when I was confirmed. I was confirmed in September of 2020, during the pandemic. Some people might say that this was unlucky, but I think the opposite. I woke up early in the morning with my whole family at home. I lived with my mom, my dad, and my 4 siblings. Since Covid was around everyone was home. My three older siblings all moved out, so having them back was really nice. Also, during this time my grandma from Italy was stuck in the US, because of the pandemic, so she was also there for my conformation. We went to church for my conformation in the morning and all was normal. After was the reason I had one of the best days. We had a mini party in our backyard after the ceremony. We had my best friend, my neighbors that were good friends, and an Italian family that were good friends all came over for the party. We had a really good time listening and singing to music, playing ping pong, and swimming in the pool. Then when we all dried up, I started opening the gifts. I wasn’t expecting much, but I was wrong. I opened the first one and it was money. I never minded getting money because I could just buy whatever I wanted. Then, I opened the second one and it was a pair of air pods, that I have been wanting for a long time. Then finally I opened the last gift. I could tell from the shape that it was going to be a special gift. It was a long rectangular box. When I opened it, my heart dropped. It was an all-black box made all of leather. I knew what it was right away because I’ve been wanting this gift for years. When I opened the box, the shine from the gold necklace I got glistened in my eyes. It was the most beautiful necklace I had ever seen. The necklace was so shiny, and it looked almost too good to wear. I put on the necklace and could tell that it was very expensive. I was not going to lose this necklace like I did to my last one. I felt almost bad for taking the gift because I knew how much it had costed my family. I knew that my family had to sacrifice to buy it, but I would’ve of ever though that thy would have bought it for me.

annotations.text: knew that my family had to sacrifice to buy it, but I would’ve of ever though that thy would have bought it for me.
annotations.comment: why was buying it for you a surprise? I'm interested!

paragraphs.body: This gold necklace was not like any other gold necklace. It has multiple all gold chains that glow like no other. It is a bright yellow color with a metallic look to it. The chain holds many links and feels cold when kept it out of my shirt and warm when kept inside my shirt. The chain holds a pendent that pops when looking at it. The pendent will making a clanking noise when moving it making it very satisfying playing with it. The pendent is of Jesus on the cross, which is all made of gold and is finely detailed. This gold necklace smells of the cologne that I put on that morning. Always smelling good gives, me confidence during the day. Also, when I’m stressed, I sometimes put the pendent in my mouth and it always taste like the cologne. The cologne doesn’t taste good, and it gives it a bitter taste, but I still do it because I can usually smell it.

annotations.text: This gold necklace smells of the cologne that I put on that morning.

annotations.comment: Nice job with description :) Just keep your verb tenses consistent.

paragraphs.body: My parents have both immigrated multiple times in their lives. My dad was born in the US and immigrated to Italy when he was twelve years old. My grandfather was sick in Chicago, and he wanted to move back to Italy to be closer to family. My mom was born in Venezuela. Her dad died when she was twelve, so her mom moved to the US with her five kids. My grandma then decided to move back to Italy, where she was born. Then when my parents met in Italy they decided to immigrate to America for a better life. My parents both had to leave a place they liked for better opportunities.

annotations.text: My parents have both immigrated multiple times in their lives. My dad was born in the US and immigrated to Italy when he was twelve years old. My grandfather was sick in Chicago, and he wanted to move back to Italy to be closer to family. My mom was born in Venezuela. Her dad died when she was twelve, so her mom moved to the US with her five kids.

paragraphs.body: When I thought, “Why would anyone leave the place they were born for another place?” I did not think about all the paths that could have happened. If I stayed in Italy then I would be having a completely different life, probably not as fortunate. In Italy as a family of seven we lived in a three-bedroom apartment. The job opportunities in Italy are terrible and a lot of young adults are leaving the country. People are not having enough babies and the people living in Italy are just getting older. My parents had to sacrifice their whole carriers to move our whole family to another country and it turned out well. They did it because they loved us our family The word love can be used in many different ways. It could be a feeling towards someone else. It could be an object that you deeply like. The word love to me is something that has great meaning to a person. When I use the word love it is not something that is generic. It is something that is special to me. It can also be a feeling that is shared. Like if two people love football. But love towards a person is a bit tougher to define. It could be a shared feeling towards two people. Someone that the person spends a lot of time with. For me when talking about loving a person, I think that it is when someone makes the other person and themselves happy. Even if someone does something that they dislike, but it makes the other person happy. The sacrifice is worth the punishment because at the end it will make both the people happy.

paragraphs.body: My mom and dad sacrificed being near their own family just for a better life for their kids. My parents showed that they love their kids by sacrificing for them. That is what true love is. That is the true meaning of life. Not buying the biggest house or the most expensive car. It is all about making other people and yourself happy. Sacrificing for the love of others is the true meaning of happiness. That is something that money can not but. Money can only get you so far, but truly loving someone is how you make yourself happy.

annotations.text: Money can only get you so far, but truly loving someone is how you make yourself happy.
annotations.comment: Interesting! Is the metaphor of the necklace related to this idea in some way?

paragraphs.body: The word sacrifice is a world that has been used for a very long time. It was used to explain Christ’s offering of himself to the world. Then in Latin where the word is used sacrificium, which is also found in old English books, it was used as the etymonline states, “act of giving up a desirable thing for a higher object or to a more pressing claim.” Another definition was found around the 1590’s where the website states, “something given up for the sake of another” (Etymonline). Also, in the 1880’s the word sacrifice was used in baseball where it states that a, “hit made by the batter not to get himself to base but to enable another player to advance” (Etymonline).

annotations.comment: I bet, if you leverage your definition of "love" you won't even need to define "sacrifice." You'll be able to _show_ sacrifice as its connected to love through really well-chosen details.

paragraphs.body: It all goes back to the original quote, “Love is not a feeling of happiness. Love is a willingness to sacrifice.” Love is not just for your happiness. You love someone that you would sacrifice for or lose something for. To help them when they are in need and spend some time with them. People are busy, but truly caring for someone is sacrificing some time to be with them. That is how a bond with someone or true love forms. “Sacrifice.” Etymology, Douglas Harper, www.etymonline.com/search?q=sacrifice.
